headline: Retrieval and agent memory tooling broke out this month as RAG, knowledge graphs, and automated ingestion entered as new techniques alongside a surge in Model Context Protocol.
body: RAG, knowledge graphs, and automated ingestion arrived as new entrants, anchored by self-improving knowledge base workflows that pair Obsidian, Claude, and NotebookLM with cron-driven ingestion and persistent memory [6]. Model Context Protocol led the rotation, now positioned to connect design systems to coding agents for coordinated software development [5]. On the inference side, speculative decoding broke out and entered alongside a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model from Xiaomi and TileRT reaching over 1,000 tokens per second through FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernels [9]. Trajectory planning and web UI also broke out, with a /goal command for Codex running an autonomous planning and self-correction loop [4].

cited_items.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
